repos.name: VideoRAG
repos.architecture: Dual-channel (Python backend + Electron frontend) with graph-driven knowledge indexing and hierarchical context encoding for video understanding

agents.autonomy: Single intelligent processing system, not multi-agent
agents.coordination: Backend-driven processing pipeline with Electron UI

diferenciais: Processes videos up to hundreds of hours on single GPU
Multi-video comparison and cross-video understanding
Achieves 60.2% accuracy on Video-MME benchmark
Distills long content into concise knowledge representations

insights: Graph-based knowledge encoding could enhance entity registry
Hierarchical context encoding pattern useful for long-horizon tasks
Adaptive retrieval strategies could improve agent context selection

repos.name: DeepInnovator
repos.architecture: Three-layer knowledge synthesis pipeline (paper analysis → grouping → cross-disciplinary synthesis) with decoupled reward-comment RL architecture

agents.coordination: Sequential idea generation with independent feedback streams

diferenciais: Decoupled reward-comment architecture (prevents reward hacking)
Recursive multi-turn interactions (5 user turns, 6 assistant turns)
Cross-disciplinary discovery of research fusion opportunities
Matches GPT-4o/Gemini-2.5 performance at smaller scale

insights: Decoupled reward system could improve agent decision validation
Discriminator pattern useful for story/spec quality validation
Multi-turn interaction model matches AIOX story development cycle
Separation of guidance from scoring prevents task gaming

repos.name: Superpowers
repos.architecture: Plugin-based skills framework with seven-phase development methodology, subagent dispatching, and dual-stage review system

agents.coordination: Subagent-driven with parallel workflows and parent-child review

diferenciais: Seven-phase systematic methodology (not ad-hoc)
Mandatory skills-as-workflows philosophy
Pre-test code deletion enforcement (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR)
Plan compliance tracking at every review stage
Multi-platform CLI support (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI)

insights: Seven-phase workflow aligns with AIOX SDC phases
Git worktree isolation pattern matches AIOX branching strategy
Granular task breakdown (2-5min) improves agent focus
Dual-stage review (spec compliance + code quality) reduces rework
Multi-platform CLI approach enables broader tool support
Plan deviation detection prevents scope creep

repos.name: Hermes Agent
repos.architecture: Self-improving agent with modular design (agent loop → terminal backends → gateway system → memory layer → skills system)
tech_stack: Multiple LLM providers (OpenRouter 200+ models, Nous, OpenAI)
diferenciais: 200+ model support via OpenRouter
Self-improvement through skill creation
Persistent memory with LLM-driven summarization
Multi-gateway deployment (7+ platforms)
Serverless execution (Modal, Daytona) with cost efficiency
Honcho dialectic user modeling for agent personalization

insights: Terminal backend abstraction enables future AIOX executor plugins
Skill persistence and self-improvement pattern useful for agent learning
Memory search + summarization could enhance agent context retrieval
Gateway pattern enables multi-channel agent activation
Cron scheduling matches AIOX workflow automation needs
User modeling across sessions supports agent personalization

repos.name: Agency-Agents
repos.architecture: Specialized agent personas organized into 12+ functional divisions (Engineering, Design, Marketing, Sales, Product, Testing) with distinctive specialization, personality, and deliverables

tools: Multi-tool deployment (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, Gemini CLI)

diferenciais: 12+ specialized agent roles (not generic)
Deep domain expertise per agent (not templated personalities)
Distinctive voice/communication style per agent
Multi-tool deployment with conversion scripts
Well-defined success metrics and deliverables per agent
Organizational structure mirrors real enterprise teams

insights: 12-division model could expand AIOX from 10 to 15+ specialized agents
Persona + deliverables pattern matches AIOX agent design
Success metrics definition improves agent accountability
Multi-tool deployment approach useful for AIOX multi-IDE support
Enterprise functional division structure applicable to larger teams

repos.name: Squad
repos.architecture: Persistent AI team framework with SDK-based routing, individual agent sessions, knowledge persistence in `.squad/` directory, and custom team tools

agents.autonomy: High with persistent knowledge across sessions
agents.coordination: Parallel execution with automatic task chaining based on results

workflows.trigger: User task or previous session breadcrumbs

diferenciais: Persistent team state in `.squad/` (inherited on repo clone)
Individual session histories per agent (no single context window)
SDK-first mode with TypeScript compilation
Automatic task chaining based on result analysis
Breadcrumb trails for session resumption
Compiled routing rules (not prompt-based selection)
Knowledge compounding across time with skill system

para_aiox.applicability: Very High (directly aligned with AIOX multi-agent model)
insights: `.squad/` pattern directly applicable to AIOX agent persistence
SDK-based routing eliminates agent selection ambiguity
Persistent session approach solves context window explosion
Automatic task chaining reduces manual handoff overhead
Individual agent history files enable knowledge compounding
Breadcrumb trails support interrupted work resumption
Compiled routing rules improve governance and auditability

gaps_identified: AIOX lacks persistent agent session storage (Squad.squad pattern)
No automatic task chaining based on result analysis (Squad feature)
Agent knowledge doesn't compound across sessions (Squad/Hermes learnings)
Limited multi-terminal backend support (Hermes has 6, AIOX has 1)
No skill auto-creation from successful executions (Hermes pattern)
Story validation uses checklist, not discriminator validation (DeepInnovator pattern)
Plan deviation detection not implemented (Superpowers feature)
Agent specialization taxonomy incomplete (Agency-Agents has 12+ divisions)
No cross-session breadcrumb trails for resumption (Squad feature)
Context encoding for long-horizon tasks limited (VideoRAG hierarchical pattern)

priority_1_high_impact.impact: Eliminates context window explosion, enables knowledge compounding
priority_1_high_impact.implementation: Store agent session history in `.aiox/sessions/{agentId}/` with auto-load on activation

priority_1_high_impact.impact: Reduces manual handoffs, enables parallel agent workflows
priority_1_high_impact.implementation: Analyze task outputs, automatically identify dependent tasks, dispatch to relevant agents

priority_1_high_impact.impact: Replaces manual checklist review with ML-based quality gates
priority_1_high_impact.implementation: Train lightweight discriminator for story/spec quality, integrate into @po validation gate

priority_1_high_impact.impact: Improves consistency, reduces rework through stricter phase gates
priority_1_high_impact.implementation: Add Brainstorming phase before Planning, add explicit Code Review phase with severity-based blocking

priority_2_medium_impact.implementation: Abstract executor interface, implement backends: Local, Docker, SSH, Modal (serverless)

priority_2_medium_impact.impact: Enables agents to improve through experience
priority_2_medium_impact.implementation: Store successful task solutions in `.aiox/skills/`, auto-load on similar tasks
Maintain `decisions.md`, orchestration logs, session checkpoints per story

priority_3_nice_to_have.implementation: Compare execution against initial plan, alert @sm on deviations
Encode task dependencies, sub-task relationships in vector space for better retrieval

priority_3_nice_to_have.impact: Enables workflows via Slack, Discord, Email
priority_3_nice_to_have.implementation: Implement gateway adapters for Slack, Discord, Email; route to agent system

architectural_principles: Agent sessions should be persistent objects (Squad model), not ephemeral
Task chaining should be automatic based on result analysis (Squad pattern)
Validation should use discriminators, not manual checklists (DeepInnovator)
Routing rules should be compiled code, not prompt-based (Squad advantages)
Knowledge should compound across sessions via skill persistence (Hermes/Squad)
Terminal backends should be abstracted for extensibility (Hermes pattern)
Agent specialization should be granular and deep (Agency-Agents model)
Work interruption should be resumable via breadcrumb trails (Squad feature)
quick_wins: Add discriminator validation to @po story gate (~3 days)
Create breadcrumb trails for interrupted work (~1 day)
Expand to 12+ agent roles with specialization taxonomy (~2 days)
